headlines: Tether hires KPMG for first full audit of USDT
SEC and CFTC publish joint crypto taxonomy: many tokens labeled 'digital commodities'
Strategy Inc (MicroStrategy) confirms 762,099 BTC holding (March 29 filing)
NYSE removes 25,000-contract cap on Bitcoin & Ether ETF options
Morgan Stanley files amended S-1 for its spot Bitcoin ETF (MSBT) as product readies for launch

articles.title: Tether hires KPMG for first full audit of USDT
articles.summary: Tether said it has engaged a Big Four firm (reported as KPMG) to perform the first full independent audit of reserves backing USDT. The move is aimed at improving transparency for the $180B+ stablecoin and easing regulatory concerns. (reported Mar 24–27, 2026).

articles.title: SEC and CFTC publish joint crypto taxonomy; many tokens labeled 'digital commodities'
articles.summary: U.S. regulators issued a joint interpretive guidance and taxonomy that classifies many major tokens — including Bitcoin and Ether — as ‘digital commodities’ instead of securities. The guidance aims to give clearer jurisdictional rules and speed institutional adoption. (SEC press release: Mar 17, 2026).
articles.source: U.S. SEC press release / industry coverage

articles.title: Strategy Inc (MicroStrategy) confirms 762,099 BTC holding in SEC filing
articles.summary: In a Form 8‑K filed Mar 30, Strategy Inc reported it held about 762,099 BTC as of March 29, 2026 and said it made no bitcoin buys or ATM share sales between March 23–29. The filing updates investors on the company’s bitcoin inventory and legal matters. (filed Mar 30, 2026).

articles.title: NYSE drops 25,000-contract cap on Bitcoin & Ether ETF options
articles.summary: NYSE Arca and related exchanges removed the prior 25,000‑contract position limit on options for major Bitcoin and Ether ETFs, opening the door to larger and more flexible ETF options trading (including FLEX-style trades). The change was made operative with an SEC waiver. (announced Mar 22, 2026).

articles.title: Morgan Stanley advances MSBT spot-Bitcoin ETF with amended S-1 filing
articles.summary: Morgan Stanley filed an amended S‑1 for its proposed spot Bitcoin ETF (ticker MSBT), refining seed-share and custody details as it moves to list on NYSE Arca. The amendment signals big banks continue racing into the spot‑BTC ETF market. (amendments filed mid–late March 2026).
articles.source: SEC filings reported by financial media

articles.title: Quarterly/options expiries drive recent volatility: large BTC & ETH options expiry cleared
articles.summary: A very large Bitcoin and Ether options expiry in late March cleared roughly $13–16 billion notional, forcing hedging flows and short-covering that added short‑term volatility across spot and altcoin markets. Traders and funds repositioned after the expiry. (market moves Mar 27–30, 2026).

articles.title: NYSE teams with Securitize to build 24/7 tokenized securities platform
articles.summary: The NYSE announced a memorandum with Securitize to develop an NYSE-linked Digital Trading Platform that would support issuer-sponsored tokenized securities, enable on‑chain settlement, and target 24/7 trading hours. This is a major step toward tokenized stocks and ETFs. (announced Mar 24, 2026).

articles.title: Ethereum rebounds near $2,070 as markets stabilize
articles.summary: Ether climbed back to roughly $2,070 in late‑March trading after recent pullbacks, leading a modest crypto market recovery as traders reacted to ETF and regulatory developments. Short‑term momentum and ETF flows are cited as drivers. (price action Mar 30, 2026).
